en: Mmm, so good. I want a cheeseburger.
ko: 음, 정말 맛있어. 치즈버거 먹고 싶어.

en: This is a special soup called pho.
You told me not to say that word.
It is delicious.
This is the food of your people.
Can I get you anything else?
Actually, we're just trying to teach our daughter
about your country.
And as I'm saying that, I'm hoping you're from Vietnam. I was born there. So was Lily.
ko: 내가 그렇게 말하면서, 네가 베트남 출신이길 바라고 있어. 나는 거기서 태어났어. 릴리도 그래.

en: Oh, it's a beautiful country. My family still lives there.
ko: 아, 정말 아름다운 나라야. 내 가족은 아직도 거기 살아.

en: I hate Vietnam.
Lily, honey, we don't hate.
I hate Vietnam.
Okay, um, we need just a second.
Lily, that was rude.
I want to go home.
No, it's important that you celebrate
ko: 아니, 너는 조상들의 문화를 기념하는 게 중요해.

en: the culture of your ancestors.
You are Vietnamese.
ko: 아니, 나는 아니야. 나는 게이야. 나는 게이야. 자기야, 아니야, 너는 게이가 아니야. 너는 그냥 혼란스러운 거야.

en: No, I'm not. I'm gay.
ko: 오, 세상에, 나한테 뭐가 잘못된 거지? 마치 내가...

en: I'm gay. Honey, no, you're not gay.
You are just confused.
Oh, my God, what is wrong with me? It's like I...
Oh, please, we have tons of lesbian friends.
ko: 나는 너희 친구들을 언급할 줄 알았어, 우리를 말고. 나는 정말 게이야. 그냥 가야 해. 아니, 아무도 어디도 못 가.

en: I thought you would reference our friends and not us. I'm so gay.
ko: 그녀가 수프를 다 먹을 때까지. 아니, 글로리아, 괜찮아.

en: We should just go. No, nobody's going anywhere
until she finishes the whole soup. No, Gloria, it's fine.
No, no, no, it's not fine.
That's the problem with this country.
Nobody cares where they come from.
ko: 아무도 자신이 어디서 왔는지 신경 쓰지 않아.

en: They just want cheeseburgers and prep school,
and they have forgotten about Colombia.
